text: My fellow Americans, we stand at a crossroads. Not between red and blue, but between hope and fear. They say our best days are behind us. I say look at the child learning to code in a library that shouldn't exist, the single mom working two jobs who still shows up to coach Little League. Our strength has never been in our buildings, but in our people. So let's build a future where every zip code offers a fair shot.

Okay, so I finally figured out why my dog stares at me when I'm on the toilet. It's not judgment. He thinks I'm guarding a very tiny, very boring treasure. And honestly? He respects the hustle. That's the look. It's, 'Nice work, human. You sit on the porcelain throne of mystery. I'll just watch the door.'

Dearly beloved, we are gathered here today not just to celebrate Becky and Tom, but to acknowledge that Tom once tried to impress Becky by reciting the entire script of 'The Princess Bride' from memory. He got through about 45 seconds before she laughed so hard she snorted her drink. And that, my friends, is when he knew. To love is to be willing to look absolutely ridiculous in front of the person you adore. Raise your glasses.

I need help writing a eulogy for my mom. She was a librarian who hated sentimentality, but she secretly loved bad romance novels and fed every stray cat in the neighborhood. I want it to be honest—funny but not disrespectful, and short. She'd hate a long speech. Can you help me start it?

Brothers and sisters, I tell you, the storm is not here to destroy you—it is here to wake you up. You've been sleeping through your own miracle. The same wind that bends the tree also scatters its seeds. So stand up! Stand up and say, 'I am not a victim of my circumstance, I am a steward of my destiny!' Let the church say amen!

Good morning, graduates. Welcome to the real world. The bad news: none of this is in the syllabus. The good news: you've already learned the only thing that matters—how to learn. Your degree is not a ticket to a job; it's a toolkit for a life. So build something weird. Build something kind. Build something that scares you a little. And for God's sake, tip your movers.

[PRESS RELEASE] FOR IMMEDIATE RELEASE: Acme Corp announces the opening of its first net-zero manufacturing facility in Toledo, Ohio. 'This plant will run entirely on renewable energy and recycle 98% of its water,' said CEO Jane Holloway. 'We are proving that profitability and sustainability are not opposing forces.' The facility is expected to create 1,200 local jobs by Q3.

My friends, what an honor it is to receive this award. I'd like to thank the Academy, of course, and also my agent, who once talked me out of quitting this business to become a llama farmer. To every kid watching who feels like a weirdo: lean into it. That weirdness is your only original asset. Mine got me here. Yours will take you somewhere even better.

I'm trying to write a campaign ad script for a city council candidate who is a former schoolteacher. The tone should be warm, not attack-heavy. Focus on her record of balancing budgets and fixing playgrounds. We have 30 seconds. Can you draft something that opens with a kid on a swing set?

Alright, alright, alright! Who's ready to get loud?! I said, WHO'S READY TO GET LOUD?! We are here tonight to take back our streets! They told us we couldn't organize, they told us we were too divided. Look around you! This is not a crowd. This is a family. And families protect each other. Now, are you with me?!
I'm struggling to write a eulogy for my grandmother. She was a gardener and always had a story for every plant. Can you help me start something that captures her quiet strength?

TED Talk outline: 'The Unseen Architecture of Trust.' Opening: a magician's trick. Body: three case studies from cybersecurity, marriage therapy, and venture capital. Closing: trust as a renewable resource.
So I asked my smart fridge, 'Am I out of milk?' and it said, 'You're out of hope, Dave.' That's when I realized AI has better comedic timing than my uncle at Thanksgiving.

Here's to the couple who argued about IKEA instructions for three hours and then built a bookshelf that leaned slightly to the left. May your marriage be as sturdy as that shelf, and may you always find the missing Allen wrench.
This November, vote for Sarah Chen. Not because she's perfect, but because she'll actually show up to the zoning board meetings. Because potholes don't fill themselves, and neither do promises.

We gather here not to mourn, but to honor a man who would have hated this many people in the same room. He'd have called it 'an infestation of sentiment.' So let's keep this brief, like his patience, and raise a glass to the bastard.

Opening statement in the case of Landry vs. State: 'Ladies and gentlemen, what we have here is not a crime of passion, but a crime of paperwork. Three signatures, two missing stamps, and one life erased.'

Acceptance speech for the Lifetime Achievement in Misdirection: 'I'd like to thank the audience for not noticing I was reading my notes upside down for the first half. And to my family, for being the one true thing I could never fake.'

Rally cry before the city council vote: 'They say we're just a bunch of angry people with signs. I say we're the ones who still believe signs matter. This park isn't just grass and benches—it's the last place our kids can fall down without someone calling the cops.'

Press release draft: 'Continuum Robotics announces a voluntary recall of all 'AssistMate 3000' units manufactured in March. Select units may interpret the phrase 'I need space' as a request for orbital trajectory calculations rather than emotional distance.'
